summary: Product owner rejects formal M7 content lock because the current map still uses a rectangular/grid-like topology under visual disguise; mature-game topology research and an authoritative map topology recovery plan are required before reconsideration.

decisions.decision: Formal M7 content lock is not approved; map topology must be researched and replaced with an authoritative non-grid topology plan before reconsideration.
Drawing irregular-looking regions over rectangular/grid topology is not accepted as a strategic map solution.
A gameplay_designer/research thread is authorized to compare mature game map topology approaches and recommend a project-specific solution.

risks.summary: Current map topology undermines player mental model, AI explanations, route/capacity semantics, and save compatibility if allowed to lock.
risks.mitigation: Block content lock and create a reviewed topology recovery task chain before M7 exit.

requested_action: Record REQUEST_CHANGES, create topology research/recovery plan, and do not start M7 exit validation until recovery validation passes and product owner reconsiders content lock.
